description: Sandbox configuration enforcement rules. 18 rules governing container security, backend selection, and runtime isolation.

rules.description: ALWAYS verify container image SHA-256 before any execution
rules.rationale: Tampered images are a primary supply chain attack vector

rules.description: NEVER allow container image pull from untrusted registries
rules.rationale: Untrusted registries may host malicious or backdoored images

rules.description: ALWAYS apply read-only root filesystem to all sandbox containers
rules.rationale: Read-only rootfs prevents persistent modification of the container filesystem

rules.description: ALWAYS drop ALL Linux capabilities and add back only the minimum required
rules.rationale: Principle of least privilege for container capabilities

rules.description: ALWAYS run sandbox containers as non-root user (UID >= 1000)
rules.rationale: Non-root execution limits damage from container compromise

rules.description: NEVER allow host PID namespace sharing
rules.rationale: Host PID sharing exposes all host processes to the sandbox

rules.description: NEVER allow host IPC or network namespace sharing
rules.rationale: Host namespace sharing breaks container isolation boundaries

rules.description: ALWAYS enforce no_new_privileges flag on sandbox processes
rules.rationale: Prevents processes from gaining additional privileges at runtime

rules.description: ALWAYS apply seccomp-bpf default profile with 44 blocked syscalls
rules.rationale: Seccomp is the primary defense against kernel exploitation from containers

rules.description: NEVER allow custom seccomp profiles that reduce security below default
rules.rationale: Custom profiles must not weaken the default security posture

rules.description: ALWAYS use Kubernetes as primary backend when available; Docker as fallback
rules.rationale: Kubernetes provides the strongest isolation (PSSR restricted, RBAC, NetworkPolicy)

rules.description: NEVER allow Docker-in-Docker (DinD) or container runtime nesting
rules.rationale: Container runtime socket access grants full host control

rules.description: ALWAYS enforce tmpfs size limits (max 1Gi for /tmp)
rules.rationale: Prevents tmpfs from consuming all available memory

rules.description: ALWAYS include security-opt=no-new-privileges in Docker run commands

rules.rationale: Writable proc/sys enables kernel parameter modification and information disclosure

rules.description: NEVER reuse sandbox containers across different skills or sessions
rules.rationale: Container reuse creates cross-contamination between untrusted workloads

rules.description: ALWAYS label sandbox pods with kimi_session_id and skill_name for tracing
rules.requirement: labels.kimi_session_id is set and labels.kimi_skill_name is set
rules.rationale: Labels enable pod tracing and audit trail correlation

rules.description: NEVER allow escape hatches or emergency bypass of sandbox isolation
rules.rationale: Any bypass mechanism, even 'emergency', permanently compromises the security model
